page.content: In the Glencore mining and metals report for the Asia-Pacific region, water usage intensity is measured in m3/tonne across operational sites, which total 5, with an average intensity of 12.3 for the years 2020, 2021, and 2022. The total water used in 2022 amounted to 1.5B INR, with a water recycling rate of 75%. The rehabilitation provisions for the year 2020 were valued at 500M INR, with a yearly overview indicating a 10% change and a 20% market share, while closure provisions for the same year totaled 300M INR, reflecting a 5% change and a steady market share. The total provisions for 2020 reached 800M INR, with a year-on-year change of 7.5% and an 18% market share. Analysis of the years 2020, 2021, and 2022 indicates an overall growth of 8.5%, with sector performance remaining stable.

page.content: The financial overview includes yearly changes, highlighting trends in market share and future outlook, crucial for understanding the operational and environmental impacts of Glencore within the Asia-Pacific mining sector.
